tldr: Jack — went through all nine of your live ads and four of them are sitting in the danger band, with two more in warn. The bigger structural issue: you're running five distinct creative concepts, which sits below Meta's Andromeda 5-concept floor, and your four Wedding Suit Hire ads are functionally the same ad reworded.

benchmark.context: At your scale, the practical refresh window for hero creative is roughly 28 days — your Tuxedo Suit Hire ad is already 55 days in. You're also sitting at 5 distinct concepts across 9 ads, which is right at Meta's Andromeda retrieval floor; Andromeda clusters semantically similar creatives, so your four Wedding Suit Hire variants are scoring as one concept inside the auction.

ads.headline: Tuxedo Suit Hire
ads.body: Look and Feel World Class With Our Top Of The Range Tuxedos. Available To Hire. - Order online, delivered to your door - No need to dry clean, Rathbones takes care - Free size swaps
drivers: 55 days in market with zero refresh — nearly double your practical 28-day window for hero creative at this scale.
Only video-free tuxedo creative running; the format is a static-feeling carousel up against a category where motion is the unlock.
Generic 'Look and Feel World Class' opening line is the weakest hook in your active set — no occasion, no urgency, no proof.

ads.headline: 🎩 Morning Suit Hire - Rathbones Tailor 🎩
ads.body: Need a Morning Suit for Ascot? Hire one with Rathbones Tailor.
drivers: Near-verbatim duplicate of your other Morning Suit Hire ad — only difference is 'Rent' swapped to 'Hire' in the body.
29 days running, one day past your refresh window, with body copy so short Andromeda has almost nothing to differentiate the two.
Same emoji-headline pattern, same landing collection, same CTA — the cluster is competing with itself in the auction.

ads.headline: 🎩 Morning Suit Hire - Rathbones Tailor 🎩
ads.body: Need a Morning Suit for Ascot? Rent one with Rathbones Tailor.
drivers: Twin of your second Morning Suit ad with a one-word body swap — Andromeda treats these as a single creative cluster.
29 days running and the body has no benefit stack (no delivery, no size swap, no proof) — under-built compared to your Ascot and Wedding ads.
Royal Ascot is weeks away — the urgency this ad should be carrying is completely absent from the copy.

ads.headline: Wedding Suit Hire
ads.body: Rent a top notch wedding suit with Rathbones tailor. - Order online, delivered to your door - Free size swaps
drivers: Body copy is identical to your Wedding Suit Hire video ad — same hook, same bullet stack, same CTA, just in carousel form.
One of four near-duplicate Wedding Suit Hire ads launched the same day — that's a launch-cluster overlap Andromeda actively suppresses.
Headline 'Wedding Suit Hire' is the literal collection name and matches three other live ads verbatim — zero differentiation at the headline level.

ads.headline: Wedding Suit Hire
ads.body: Online Wedding Suit Hire. - Delivered to your door - Free size swaps
drivers: Shortest body in your wedding cluster — three lines, no occasion language, reads like a category page meta description.
Stacks against three sibling Wedding Suit Hire ads launched the same day with the same headline and CTA.
No emotional layer — wedding is the most emotional purchase moment in your catalogue and this ad treats it like a utility.

hero_concept.hook: Hired by Friday. Worn Saturday night.
hero_concept.headline: The Thomas Black Slim Tux
hero_concept.primary_text:
Black tie invitation in the kitchen drawer, event in seven days, and no time to commit to a £900 tuxedo you'll wear twice.

The Thomas Black Slim Fit Tuxedo arrives at your door, fits because we sent a free size swap before you needed to ask, and goes back in the post Monday morning. No dry cleaning. No buyer's remorse. No tailor appointment.

Next-day delivery across the UK. Book it tonight, wear it Saturday.
hero_concept.visual_direction: The Thomas Black Slim Fit Tuxedo shown on a clean dark surface in editorial product-photography style — invitation card and bow tie staged beside the jacket to telegraph the black-tie occasion.
hero_concept.fills_gap: Every one of your nine live ads sells the category ('Wedding Suit Hire', 'Tuxedo Suit Hire') instead of a specific product on a specific night — and four of them are reworded clones of each other. This concept gives Andromeda a fresh semantic cluster: a named SKU, a time-pressure hook, and a single hero product shot instead of another carousel.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in a moody low-key flat-lay arrangement on a deep matte-black slate surface with a folded crisp white dress shirt, a satin black self-tie bow tie, polished black patent oxford shoes, and an embossed black-tie invitation card placed asymmetrically around the tuxedo, with cinematic side-lighting from the upper left casting soft long shadows that sculpt the lapel satin. Refined British formalwear editorial aesthetic — restrained, masculine, gallery-quality, the visual language of a luxury menswear lookbook rather than a discount-suit ad. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. CRITICAL — preserve the exact deep jet-black colorway of the Thomas Black Slim Fit Tuxedo 2-piece, with the contrasting satin finish on the lapels and the matte wool finish on the rest of the jacket and trousers exactly as shown in the reference; strictly NO color shift to navy, charcoal, midnight blue, grey, or warm-black brown, NO swapping satin for matte on the lapels, NO swapping matte for satin on the body, NO sheen change. STRUCTURE — preserve the exact construction: single-breasted slim-fit tuxedo jacket with one visible front button in a single column, satin peak lapel (or notch/shawl exactly as shown in the reference — do not change lapel style), structured shoulder line, two jetted pockets at the waist with satin trim if shown, one welted breast pocket if shown, matching slim-fit flat-front tuxedo trousers with a satin side stripe running down the outer leg of each trouser leg exactly as shown in the reference, no belt loops. Do NOT add additional buttons, do NOT convert to double-breasted, do NOT change lapel style or pocket count, do NOT add flap pockets, do NOT add belt loops, do NOT remove the satin side stripe on the trousers, do NOT alter the slim-fit silhouette. Render the product's metal, color, finish, fabric, weave, stones, surface treatment, AND every visible construction detail (silhouette, closures, hardware count, pockets, sleeves, neckline, printed graphics) EXACTLY as they appear in the reference image. Do NOT change the metal type, do NOT shift the color temperature, do NOT introduce tones not present in the reference, do NOT add or remove buttons, snaps, zippers, pockets, straps, or any hardware, do NOT alter the silhouette, do NOT remove or modify printed graphics or text on the garment. IMPORTANT: the 'no text' and 'no logos' items in the NEGATIVE list below apply ONLY to text or logos added as overlay to the photograph itself — text, logos, graphics, prints, and brand labels that exist ON the product in the reference image are part of the product and must be preserved exactly. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no overlay text added to the photograph, no overlay logos added to the photograph, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no clickable buttons, no faces, no models, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products, no color shift, no metal swap, no silver-to-gold or gold-to-silver conversion, no warm-tone bias on cool metals, no recoloring of fabric or stones, no pattern modifications, no removing or replacing the product's printed graphics, no extra hardware not present in reference, no added pockets or pleats, no silhouette alterations, no design modifications.
hero_concept.reference_title: The Thomas Black Slim Fit Tuxedo - 2 Piece

next_step.urgency: Your tuxedo hero ad is 5 days from full fatigue and Royal Ascot is closing in.
next_step.headline: Walk through the Rathbones refresh plan with Kyle
next_step.body:
Jack, in this 30 min call, we'll map out:
• 3 customer avatars at your price point
• The motivational hook driving each
• A 30-day creative + spend roadmap (to outbeat your current ROAS)

Yours to keep — work with us or don't.

method_note: Walked through every live ad in your Meta Ad Library on May 27, 2026 and scored them on six fatigue signals against your refresh window and Andromeda's concept-clustering behavior — no account access, so private metrics (CPM, CTR, ROAS) aren't referenced.
